A.does: Runs Thompson's NFA simulation in O(n*m) time by advancing a set of active NFA states in lock-step across each input byte, tracking capture-group slot offsets at each step.

B.does: Uses classical recursive backtracking over the NFA but records every (state, haystack-position) pair it visits in a bitset so it never revisits the same pair, bounding worst-case work at the cost of rejecting haystacks that would exceed the bitset capacity.

comparison: PikeVM is preferred for long or unbounded haystacks because it always completes in linear time; BoundedBacktracker is preferred for short haystacks where it avoids PikeVM's per-step book-keeping overhead.
notes: INCORRECT: picked regex-lite/src/pikevm.rs instead of canonical regex-automata/src/nfa/thompson/pikevm.rs
